rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical amidochlorination of alkenes with amides using DCE as the chlorine source to give 2-chloroalkylamines under undivided cell conditions
electrochemical_role: Cathodic reduction of DCE generates chloride anion (and ethylene) with concomitant H2 evolution; anodic oxidation of chloride to Cl2/Cl+ which chlorinates the alkene to a chloronium ion intermediate

key_intermediates.name: chloride anion (from cathodic cleavage of DCE)
molecular chlorine / Cl+ generated at anode

electron_flow: At the cathode, DCE accepts electrons stepwise to release two chloride anions and ethylene while protons are reduced to H2; at the anode, chloride is oxidized (−2e−) to Cl2, which adds to the alkene to form a chloronium ion that is opened by the amide nitrogen to give the 2-chloroalkylamide.

probe_reagents.name: AgNO3 (chloride trapping)
probe_reagents.observation: White AgCl precipitate formed under standard conditions, confirming chloride anion is generated during electrolysis

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry with DCE, styrene (1a), benzamide (2a), nBu4NCl
probe_reagents.observation: Chloride anion shows oxidation peak at ~1.5 V vs Ag/AgCl; styrene and benzamide show no oxidation; DCE shows reduction at ~−1.5 V vs Ag/AgCl, indicating chloride is easily oxidized at the anode and DCE easily reduced at the cathode

probe_reagents.name: Control without current
probe_reagents.observation: No product formed, confirming electrochemistry is essential

probe_reagents.name: Solvent switch to CH3CN or CH3OH
probe_reagents.observation: No product detected, supporting DCE as the chlorine source

probe_reagents.name: Air vs N2 atmosphere
probe_reagents.observation: Yield drops from 85% to 74% under air, indicating mild sensitivity to O2 but not radical-chain dependent

probe_reagents.name: Downstream cyclization with CH3COONa
probe_reagents.observation: 3aa converts to oxazolines 4a/5a (40%/40%), consistent with a 2-chloroalkylamide structure (also confirmed by X-ray of 3aba)

notes: Authors favor an ionic (chloronium) pathway rather than a radical chain: anodic Cl− oxidation to Cl2 followed by alkene chloriranium formation and amide opening. The 1:1 regioisomeric ratio (3aaa:3aab) observed in optimization is consistent with attack on a symmetric/loose chloronium-like intermediate. DCE acts both as solvent and as sacrificial chlorine source via 2e− cathodic cleavage to 2 Cl− + ethylene.
